source: Water Dragon Classic (Shui Long Jing), Form School, Flying Stars

rules.description: Water feature should be placed at the front of the building (ming tang)
rules.notes: Water in the bright hall gathers prosperity qi. Avoid placing water behind the building.

rules.description: Water behind the building is inauspicious
rules.notes: Water behind weakens the Tortoise support. Exception: very large bodies of water (lake/ocean) at a distance can serve as a secondary bright hall.

rules.description: Water should flow toward the building, not away from it
rules.notes: Water flowing away carries wealth qi with it. Rivers curving toward and embracing the building are ideal (jade belt water).

rules.description: Water should flow gently, not rush violently past the property
rules.notes: Rushing water creates sha qi and carries wealth past too quickly. Slow, meandering flow is auspicious.

rules.description: Stagnant or dirty water is sha — must be clean and moving
rules.notes: Stagnant water generates yin sha and illness qi. All water features must be maintained, clean, and circulating.

rules.description: Aquariums and indoor water features best placed in north, east, or southeast sectors
rules.notes: North = water element native sector. Southeast = wealth sector enhanced by water. East = wood sector fed by water.

rules.description: Do not place water features in the bedroom
rules.notes: Water in bedroom creates excessive yin energy, disrupts sleep and can cause relationship issues.

rules.description: Do not place water features in the south sector
rules.notes: South is fire element — water here creates destructive fire-water clash.

rules.description: Fish tank auspicious number of fish: 1, 6, 7, 8, or 9
rules.notes: 8 is most popular (prosperity). Common combo: 8 gold/red + 1 black (absorbs sha). 9 symbolizes completeness.

rules.description: Swimming pool should be in front or side of building, not behind
rules.notes: Same principle as general water placement — water in front gathers qi.

rules.description: Pool shape should be kidney/curved embracing the building, not angled
rules.notes: Curved pool embracing the building creates jade belt water effect. Rectangular is neutral. L-shaped pools pointing away are unfavorable.

rules.description: Pool should not be larger than the building footprint
rules.notes: Excessively large water relative to building creates yin-yang imbalance — water overwhelms the structure.

rules.description: Toilet should not be in the southeast (wealth) sector
rules.notes: Flushing in the wealth sector drains financial qi. If unavoidable, keep lid closed and door shut.

rules.description: Toilet should not be in the center of the building
rules.notes: Center is tai qi — toilet here poisons the heart of the home. Worst placement possible.

rules.description: Visible drains and exposed plumbing should be covered
rules.notes: Visible drains represent visible wealth leaking. Cover floor drains, conceal pipes.

rules.description: Keep all toilet lids closed when not in use
rules.notes: Open toilets continuously drain qi. Simple habit that preserves wealth energy.
